title: luzula arctica subsp. latifolia
intro: Luzula arctica subsp. latifolia, also known as Arctic woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Arctic tundra, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparations of Luzula arctica subsp. latifolia include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate congestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being.
